games.title: Esserman – Van Wely, 2011
games.description:
"Marc of Excellence"
Esserman, Marc - Van Wely, Loek
112th U.S. Open, 2011
Paulsen Formation (Ch. 6)

The Morra Gambit is widely considered dubious at the highest levels of competitive chess. In this remarkable encounter, IM Marc Esserman challenges that "consensus" against 2700-rated GM Loek van Wely.

With meticulous preparation, Esserman unleashes the thematic sacrifice 11. Nd5. The Black king is stranded in the center after 13. d6! and a single inaccuracy 15...Qf6?! is all it takes for Black's position to collapse.

This 25-move miniature demonstrates the Morra's depth and attacking potential.

games.title: Nakamura – Caruana, 2023
games.description:
"Once in a Blue Morra"
Nakamura, Hikaru - Caruana, Fabiano
Chessable Masters 2023, online rapid
Paulsen Formation (Ch. 6)

It is a rare sight to see players of this calibre play the Morra in high-stakes games, even at rapid time controls. In the Grand Final of Chessable Masters 2023, Nakamura employed it twice against Caruana — winning this game and losing the other.

14. Nh4! is played after ...h6, targeting f5 and the freshly weakened g6-square; 15. Ng6 duly follows. By 17. Bxe6+, White's bishop pair provides sufficient compensation and the game goes on.

games.title: Smith – Mecking, 1972
games.description:
"Three Strikes"
Smith, Kenneth - Mecking, Henrique
San Antonio 1972
Classical Formation (Ch. 1)

Ken Smith, the "Smith" in Smith-Morra, proudly put his own theory into practice against the world's best at San Antonio 1972. By this stage of the tournament, he had already suffered two defeats with the gambit. Undeterred, he played it again.

After 12...Nxd5, recapturing with the bishop would leave White with, adequate or not, positional compensation for the pawn. Instead, 13. exd5 cedes the outpost. If allowed to complete his development, Black stands clearly better. Out of desperation, Smith lashes out with 14. Nxe5?! However, with precise defense, Mecking manages to consolidate and Smith has to resign.

For all the class difference, the brevity of the defeat says less about Smith than about the gambit. The pressure to justify the pawn sometimes leads to premature attack.

games.title: Fischer – Korchnoi, 1960
games.description:
"First and Last"
Fischer, Robert James - Korchnoi, Viktor
Buenos Aires 1960
Classical Formation (Ch. 1)

Buenos Aires 1960 marked Fischer's only Morra Gambit in classical tournaments and his first encounter with Korchnoi. The point is split after 29 moves.

games.title: Kieseritzky – Vitzthum von Eckstaedt, 1846
games.description:
"The Bonnie Early Morra"
Kieseritzky, Lionel - Vitzthum von Eckstaedt, Conrad
casual game, Paris 1846
Paulsen Formation (Ch. 6)

Played in the Romantic era, this 1846 game is considered the first recorded Morra Gambit. The "Immortal Game" is remembered for Kieseritzky's defeat as Black; here, he plays well with the white pieces.

Both sides produce suboptimal moves in the opening phase, but the ensuing tactics are decent. And the early mistakes highlight the chaos that accepting the gambit often conjures.
